description: The 'Antique' Shift: Pick an object, then refer to it by an outdated name (e.g., 'pocket telegraph' for phone, 'davenport' for sofa) for the rest of the round.
The Unearned Expert: Pick a boring topic (e.g., history of cardboard) and pretend to be an obsessive enthusiast for at least 90 seconds.
The 'Parental' Reflex: Treat players like children (e.g., ask 'Did you wash your hands?' or 'Do you need a bathroom break?') then return to normal conversation.
The Slow Clapper: Start a slow, rhythmic clap after someone finishes a sentence or a drink.
The Sommelier of Water: Swirl your water glass, sniff it deeply, and describe its 'earthy notes' to whoever is nearest.
The Echo: Repeat the last three words of someone else’s sentence under your breath, twice.
The Safety Inspector: Before sitting down, pick up the chair and inspect the bottom for a 'safety certification' mark.
The Human Statue: During a conversation, freeze completely still for exactly 15 seconds, then resume as if nothing happened.
The Corporate Pivot: Use at least five corporate buzzwords (e.g., 'synergy', 'touch base', 'low-hanging fruit') in a single story.
The Mirror: Subtly but clearly mimic the exact physical posture of the person you are talking to for two full minutes.
The Invisible Barrier: Act as if there is an invisible wall between two pieces of furniture, refusing to walk between them.
The One-Minute Delay: Answer a direct question exactly 60 seconds after it was asked.
The Thumb War: Attempt to initiate a thumb war with someone during a serious or quiet moment.
The Nickname Generator: Give two different people nicknames based on tropical fruits and only use those names for the round.
The Yoga Master: Incorporate one obvious yoga pose (like 'Tree Pose') into a standing conversation for 30 seconds.
The Sneeze Maestro: Sneeze three times, making each sneeze sound increasingly like a different musical note.
The Imaginary Buddy: Reference a mutual 'friend' named Bartholomew and act surprised when others say they don't know him.
The Sound Effect: Make a distinct 'whoosh' or 'boop' sound every time you sit down or stand up.
The Air Quotes: Use 'air quotes' around words that definitely don't require them (e.g., 'I’m going to the "kitchen" to get some "water"').
The Formal Bow: Give a deep, Victorian-style bow to anyone who performs a small favor for you (like passing a snack).
The Flavor Profile: Ask someone what 'color' their food or drink tastes like, then disagree with their answer.
The Shadow Boxer: After making a point in a conversation, perform three quick, silent punches into the air.
The Time Traveler: Refer to a common modern object (like a microwave or TV) as 'primitive technology' or 'a classic relic.'
The Background Music: Hum a suspenseful, movie-trailer style theme song while another player is telling a story.
The Hype Man: Introduce a player to someone they already know as if they are a world-famous celebrity.
The Acronym Enthusiast: Invent an acronym for a common object (e.g., 'Pass me the B.O.S.' for Bottle of Soda) and refuse to explain it.
The Ghost Hunter: Suddenly stop, look at a corner of the room, and ask if anyone else 'felt that cold spot.'
The Professional Handshaker: Shake someone's hand for a full 10 seconds while maintaining intense eye contact.
The Third Person: Refer to yourself in the third person for the duration of a single story or explanation.
The Prop Master: Pick up a kitchen utensil (like a whisk or spatula) and carry it with you as a 'talking stick' for 5 minutes.
The Weather Reporter: Provide a detailed 5-day weather forecast for a city that is at least 1,000 miles away.
The Silent Librarian: Shush the entire room loudly when the conversation is already at a reasonable volume.
The Pocket Sand: Pretend to throw 'invisible sand' at someone to 'distract them' while you walk away from a conversation.
The Fashion Police: Give a player a genuine, high-energy compliment on their 'brave and avant-garde' choice of socks.
The Slow Motion: Perform one entire task (like pouring a drink or putting on a jacket) in exaggerated slow motion.
The Fortune Teller: Predict a very mundane event (e.g., 'Someone will say the word 'hungry' in the next 3 minutes') and celebrate if it happens.
The Director: Tell two people to 'reset the scene' and ask them to repeat their last interaction because the 'lighting was wrong.'
The Compliment Sandwich: Give someone a compliment, followed by a confusingly specific insult, followed by another compliment.
The Historical Footnote: Correct someone on a completely fabricated 'historical fact' about the house or building you are currently in.
The Animal Kingdom: Compare three different players to specific breeds of dogs and explain your reasoning in detail.
The Mime: Act as if you are stuck in an invisible box for exactly five seconds, then immediately stop and act normal.
The Unsolicited Advice: Give someone 'expert' advice on a better way to hold their phone or drink for 'maximum ergonomic efficiency.'
The Missing Item: Ask the group if anyone has seen your 'emotional support stapler' (or another non-existent weird object).
The Aggressive Tipper: Try to hand someone a coin, bill, or token as a 'tip' for a very small favor, like passing the salt.
The Looming Presence: Stand slightly too close to a player (within their personal bubble) for 10 seconds, then walk away without a word.
The Inconspicuous Re-aligner: Identify a set of objects (coasters, chairs, pens) and slowly, over 10 minutes, align them perfectly parallel to one another.
